text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously constructive - oil's biggest quarterly drop in six years eases inflation fears, which is generally good for rate-sensitive growth and tech. No major macro shock in today's headlines, so semis and chip names should hold their tone. The AI drug-discovery angle (Anthropic) keeps the broader AI narrative alive, which underpins your book.

Your holdings: Everything is on HOLD and all positions are in the green - no immediate fires. SOXL (+4.8%) is a 3x leveraged ETF that decays daily; on margin that's a double-amplifier if semis reverse hard - keep your $200 stop honest. SPAL is brand-new and very volatile - moves fast and decays; on margin even a small gap down stings. QQQI (+0.67%) is barely moving - not a concern but worth knowing it's your deadest weight right now.

Watch: (1) Semiconductor sentiment - any wobble in NVDA or SMH leadership will hit SOXL and SMH hard given your leverage. (2) Whether the AI catalyst (Anthropic news) pulls chip names higher intraday.

Bottom line: Your book is clean and green - protect the leveraged positions with hard stops and don't chase new buys into premium without a clear trigger.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is wide - consider tightening toward $220-230 given 3x leverage on margin; a 10% SMH drop wipes ~30% here fast.
SPAL: New and volatile - confirm your $21.38 stop is live; no reason to add until it proves itself with a few days of price action.

No new buys today - scanner setups for ALAB (best score, 9/12, entry ~$482) are interesting but price is already in daily premium meaning you'd be chasing; wait for a pullback to confirm before touching it on margin.
